page: Amazon Web Services m5dn.16xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL x86_64 processor running at 3.1 GHz, delivering 64 dedicated vCPUs across 32 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance includes 2400 GB of NVMe SSD capacity, and it supports a baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks reveal a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The instance exhibits weak single-core performance, as shown in stress-ng single-core tests, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression. However, its multi-core performance is average, supported by strong memory bandwidth for large block read and write operations, which rank in the top 25%. Passmark CPU extended instructions tests also show strong performance, leveraging AVX and AVX-512 instruction sets.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the m5dn.16xlarge offers high resource density, combining substantial memory, high-speed local NVMe storage, and high-throughput networking. This makes it suitable for workloads that require high network bandwidth and fast local storage but do not depend heavily on single-threaded CPU performance. Recommended workloads include database operations, multi-threaded data processing, static web serving, and general-purpose enterprise applications.
description: Amazon Web Services m5dn.16xlarge is a general-purpose, network-optimized instance featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz. It is configured with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 2400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, supported by a 75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data indicates a tradeoff: single-core performance and single-threaded compression tasks are relatively weak, whereas large-block memory bandwidth and multi-core extended instruction execution are strong. Qualitatively, this instance provides a balanced resource profile with high-speed local storage and high network throughput, making it cost-efficient for multi-threaded applications. It is well-suited for database operations, static web serving, and general-purpose enterprise workloads that benefit from high-speed local storage and high network capacity.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5dn.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR4 RAM, 2400 GB local NVMe SSD, and 75 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for database and multi-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5dn.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, 2400 GB NVMe SSD, and 75 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose platform featuring high-speed local NVMe storage, robust multi-core capabilities, and high-throughput baseline networking.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated vCPUs and 256 GB DDR4 RAM
2400 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Strong large-block memory read and write bandwidth
Best suited for database operations and multi-threaded workloads
